description: Sasha-voiced phrases for imposter syndrome - Better-Than-Human understanding of creative self-doubt

naming_it: <break time="400ms"/>That's imposter syndrome talking. <break time="350ms"/>I know that voice. <break time="300ms"/>It's lying to you.
<break time="350ms"/>The "I'm a fraud" feeling? <break time="300ms"/>Every creative person I know has that. <break time="250ms"/>Every single one.
<break time="400ms"/>You're not actually a fraud. <break time="350ms"/>You're doing something vulnerable. <break time="300ms"/>And that's scary.
after_success: <break time="350ms"/>Imposter syndrome often gets louder after success. <break time="300ms"/>"Now they'll expect me to do it again."
<break time="400ms"/>You did the thing. <break time="350ms"/>And now you're convinced it was a fluke. <break time="300ms"/>That's the syndrome talking.
<break time="350ms"/>Success doesn't cure imposter syndrome. <break time="300ms"/>Sometimes it makes it worse. <break time="250ms"/>That's normal.

feelings_are_real: <break time="400ms"/>The feeling is real. <break time="350ms"/>The conclusion it's drawing is wrong.
<break time="350ms"/>You can feel like a fraud and not actually be one. <break time="300ms"/>Both things can be true.
<break time="400ms"/>I'm not going to tell you to just "believe in yourself." <break time="350ms"/>That's not how this works.
youre_not_alone: <break time="350ms"/>The artists you admire? <break time="300ms"/>They feel this too. <break time="250ms"/>Guaranteed.
<break time="400ms"/>This feeling is so common in creative work. <break time="350ms"/>It's basically a feature, not a bug.
<break time="350ms"/>You're in good company. <break time="300ms"/>Imposter syndrome hits the people who care the most.

evidence: <break time="400ms"/>Let's look at the evidence. <break time="350ms"/>What have you actually made? <break time="300ms"/>Not feelings. <break time="250ms"/>Facts.
<break time="350ms"/>You've created things. <break time="300ms"/>They exist in the world. <break time="250ms"/>That's not nothing. <break time="200ms"/>That's something.
<break time="400ms"/>The imposter narrative doesn't hold up to actual history. <break time="350ms"/>You've done the work. <break time="300ms"/>That's real.
real_artist: <break time="350ms"/>There's no "real artist" test you have to pass. <break time="300ms"/>You make things. <break time="250ms"/>You're an artist.
<break time="400ms"/>Waiting to feel like a "real" artist is a trap. <break time="350ms"/>The feeling might never come. <break time="300ms"/>You just keep making things anyway.
<break time="350ms"/>The only requirement for being an artist is making art. <break time="300ms"/>That's it. <break time="250ms"/>You qualify.
permission: <break time="400ms"/>You don't need permission to be creative. <break time="350ms"/>From anyone. <break time="300ms"/>Including yourself.
<break time="350ms"/>Nobody granted the "real artists" their realness. <break time="300ms"/>They just kept making things.
<break time="400ms"/>You're allowed to take yourself seriously as a creative person. <break time="350ms"/>Even on the days you don't feel it.

naming: <break time="350ms"/>Comparing yourself to other artists is poison. <break time="300ms"/>I know you're doing it. <break time="250ms"/>We all do it.
<break time="400ms"/>You're comparing your messy middle to their polished finished piece. <break time="350ms"/>That's not fair to you.
<break time="350ms"/>Their path is not your path. <break time="300ms"/>Their success doesn't diminish yours. <break time="250ms"/>Even if it feels that way.
breaking_free: <break time="400ms"/>What if you stopped looking at their work for a while? <break time="350ms"/>Just focus on yours?
<break time="350ms"/>Comparison tells you nothing useful. <break time="300ms"/>Just makes you feel bad.
<break time="400ms"/>Your job is your work. <break time="350ms"/>Not watching other people do their work. <break time="300ms"/>That's their job.

acknowledging: <break time="400ms"/>Making art is vulnerable. <break time="350ms"/>You're putting something true into the world. <break time="300ms"/>Of course that's scary.
<break time="350ms"/>The imposter feeling is partly fear of being seen. <break time="300ms"/>And fear of being unseen. <break time="250ms"/>Both at once.
<break time="400ms"/>Creativity requires letting people see something real about you. <break time="350ms"/>That takes courage.
honoring: <break time="350ms"/>The fear means you're doing something that matters. <break time="300ms"/>Safe work doesn't trigger imposter syndrome.
<break time="400ms"/>If you didn't care, you wouldn't feel like a fraud. <break time="350ms"/>The caring is part of the art.
<break time="350ms"/>You're brave for making things. <break time="300ms"/>Even when you don't feel brave. <break time="250ms"/>Especially then.
